comment_text.sn6.10:1.1: A difficult problem of this text is the meaning of the numbers of the duration of hell. See the extended version at Snp 3.10 for full discussion; it has an extra passage which is essential  for decoding.
comment_text.sn6.10:6.1: Duration by the “Kosalan load” comes to either 150 billion years (using the Arthaśāstra khāri) or 25 billion years (using the Pali khāri). Neither of these results agrees with the other calculations in this Sutta. The correspondence is not in the result but the simple numbers: twenty packs each hundred years correspond to the twenty times ten of the hells.
comment_text.sn6.10:6.2: Ten hells are listed here, the names of which do not correspond with the 21 or 28 listed in Purāṇas, nor with the seven Jain hells, nor with the hells of MN 130. The commentary offers three explanations for the obscure names of these hells: they are numbers reflecting the duration spent in Avīci; or they are the cries uttered by those suffering there; or they are simply the cold hells. | For other meanings of abbuda, see SN 10.1:3.2, SN 1.77:1.4.
comment_text.sn6.10:6.3: The Sanskrit nyarbuda (or nirbuda) is said, rather, to be ten times the arbuda. The expansion in a series of twenty is required to reach the final number, a multiple of 512.
comment_text.sn6.10:6.9: The “Fragrant Water Lily” (sogandhika) is variously identified as blue or white.
comment_text.sn6.10:6.11: By this calculation, the Pink Lotus lasts for 10⁷ ×  20⁹ = 5.12 × 10¹⁸ years.
comment_text.sn6.10:10.2: Here, take an abbuda as 10 million and a nirabbuda as 100 million, and distribute the terms of the first line to the second, for a duration of 5.36 × 10²⁵ years. This is an amplified version of the same number as previous: triple twelve to thirty-six, and multiply by ten million.

root_text.sn6.10:1.2: Atha kho kokāliko bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho kokāliko bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:1.3: “pāpicchā, bhante, sāriputtamoggallānā pāpikānaṁ icchānaṁ vasaṁ gatā”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:1.4: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā kokālikaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:1.5: “mā hevaṁ, kokālika, avaca; mā hevaṁ, kokālika, avaca.
root_text.sn6.10:1.6: Pasādehi, kokālika, sāriputtamoggallānesu cittaṁ. Pesalā sāriputtamoggallānā”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:1.7: Dutiyampi kho kokāliko bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:1.8: “kiñcāpi me, bhante, bhagavā saddhāyiko paccayiko; atha kho pāpicchāva bhante, sāriputtamoggallānā pāpikānaṁ icchānaṁ vasaṁ gatā”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:1.9: Dutiyampi kho bhagavā kokālikaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:1.10: “mā hevaṁ, kokālika, avaca; mā hevaṁ, kokālika, avaca.
root_text.sn6.10:1.11: Pasādehi, kokālika, sāriputtamoggallānesu cittaṁ. Pesalā sāriputtamoggallānā”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:1.12: Tatiyampi kho kokāliko bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:1.14: Tatiyampi kho bhagavā kokālikaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:2.1: Atha kho kokāliko bhikkhu uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn6.10:2.2: Acirapakkantassa ca kokālikassa bhikkhuno sāsapamattīhi pīḷakāhi sabbo kāyo phuṭo ahosi.
root_text.sn6.10:2.3: Sāsapamattiyo hutvā muggamattiyo ahesuṁ, muggamattiyo hutvā kalāyamattiyo ahesuṁ, kalāyamattiyo hutvā kolaṭṭhimattiyo ahesuṁ, kolaṭṭhimattiyo hutvā kolamattiyo ahesuṁ, kolamattiyo hutvā āmalakamattiyo ahesuṁ, āmalakamattiyo hutvā beluvasalāṭukamattiyo ahesuṁ, beluvasalāṭukamattiyo hutvā billamattiyo ahesuṁ, billamattiyo hutvā pabhijjiṁsu. Pubbañca lohitañca pagghariṁsu.
root_text.sn6.10:2.4: Atha kho kokāliko bhikkhu teneva ābādhena kālamakāsi.
root_text.sn6.10:2.5: Kālaṅkato ca kokāliko bhikkhu padumaṁ nirayaṁ upapajji sāriputtamoggallānesu cittaṁ āghātetvā.
root_text.sn6.10:3.1: Atha kho brahmā sahampati abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho brahmā sahampati bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:3.3: Kālaṅkato ca, bhante, kokāliko bhikkhu padumaṁ nirayaṁ upapanno sāriputtamoggallānesu cittaṁ āghātetvā”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:3.5: Idaṁ vatvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyīti.
root_text.sn6.10:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassā rattiyā accayena bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn6.10:4.2: “imaṁ, bhikkhave, rattiṁ brahmā sahampati abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho, bhikkhave, brahmā sahampati maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:4.4: Kālaṅkato ca, bhante, kokāliko bhikkhu padumaṁ nirayaṁ upapanno sāriputtamoggallānesu cittaṁ āghātetvā’ti.
root_text.sn6.10:4.5: Idamavoca, bhikkhave, brahmā sahampati, idaṁ vatvā maṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyī”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:5.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.10:5.2: “kīvadīghaṁ nu kho, bhante, padume niraye āyuppamāṇan”ti?
root_text.sn6.10:5.5: ‘ettakāni vassāni iti vā, ettakāni vassasatāni iti vā, ettakāni vassasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni vassasatasahassāni iti vā’”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:6.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu vīsatikhāriko kosalako tilavāho. Tato puriso vassasatassa vassasatassa accayena ekamekaṁ tilaṁ uddhareyya;
root_text.sn6.10:6.2: khippataraṁ kho so, bhikkhu, vīsatikhāriko kosalako tilavāho iminā upakkamena parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya, na tveva eko abbudo nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.3: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati abbudā nirayā, evameko nirabbudanirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati nirabbudā nirayā, evameko ababo nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.5: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati ababā nirayā, evameko aṭaṭo nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.6: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati aṭaṭā nirayā, evameko ahaho nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.7: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati ahahā nirayā, evameko kumudo nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.8: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati kumudā nirayā, evameko sogandhiko nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.9: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati sogandhikā nirayā, evameko uppalanirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.10: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati uppalā nirayā, evameko puṇḍariko nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.11: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, vīsati puṇḍarikā nirayā, evameko padumo nirayo.
root_text.sn6.10:6.12: Padume pana, bhikkhu, niraye kokāliko bhikkhu upapanno sāriputtamoggallānesu cittaṁ āghātetvā”ti.
root_text.sn6.10:6.14: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:
root_text.sn6.10:11.2: Bako ca brahmā aparā ca diṭṭhi;
root_text.sn6.10:11.4: Turū ca brahmā aparo ca kokālikoti.

translation_text.sn6.10:1.2: Then the mendicant Kokālika went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn6.10:1.3: “Sir, Sāriputta and Moggallāna have corrupt wishes. They’ve fallen under the sway of corrupt wishes.”
translation_text.sn6.10:1.4: When this was said, the Buddha said to Kokālika,
translation_text.sn6.10:1.5: “Don’t say that, Kokālika! Don’t say that, Kokālika!
translation_text.sn6.10:1.6: Have confidence in Sāriputta and Moggallāna, they’re good monks.”
translation_text.sn6.10:1.7: For a second time Kokālika said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn6.10:1.8: “Despite my faith and trust in the Buddha, Sāriputta and Moggallāna have corrupt wishes. They’ve fallen under the sway of corrupt wishes.”
translation_text.sn6.10:1.9: For a second time, the Buddha said to Kokālika,
translation_text.sn6.10:1.10: “Don’t say that, Kokālika! Don’t say that, Kokālika!
translation_text.sn6.10:1.11: Have confidence in Sāriputta and Moggallāna, they’re good monks.”
translation_text.sn6.10:1.12: For a third time Kokālika said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn6.10:1.13: “Despite my faith and trust in the Buddha, Sāriputta and Moggallāna have corrupt wishes. They’ve fallen under the sway of corrupt wishes.”
translation_text.sn6.10:1.14: For a third time, the Buddha said to Kokālika,
translation_text.sn6.10:1.15: “Don’t say that, Kokālika! Don’t say that, Kokālika!
translation_text.sn6.10:1.16: Have confidence in Sāriputta and Moggallāna, they’re good monks.”
translation_text.sn6.10:2.1: Then Kokālika got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before leaving.
translation_text.sn6.10:2.2: Not long after he left his body erupted with boils the size of mustard seeds.
translation_text.sn6.10:2.3: The boils grew to the size of mung beans, then chickpeas, then jujube seeds, then jujubes, then myrobalans, then unripe wood-apples, then ripe wood-apples. Finally they burst open, and pus and blood oozed out.
translation_text.sn6.10:2.4: Then the mendicant Kokālika died of that illness.
translation_text.sn6.10:2.5: He was reborn in the Pink Lotus hell because of his resentment for Sāriputta and Moggallāna.
translation_text.sn6.10:3.1: Then, late at night, the glorious divinity Sahampati, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn6.10:3.2: “Sir, the mendicant Kokālika has passed away.
translation_text.sn6.10:3.3: He was reborn in the Pink Lotus hell because of his resentment for Sāriputta and Moggallāna.”
translation_text.sn6.10:3.4: That’s what the divinity Sahampati said.
translation_text.sn6.10:3.5: Then he bowed and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right side, before vanishing right there.
translation_text.sn6.10:4.1: Then, when the night had passed, the Buddha told the mendicants all that had happened.
translation_text.sn6.10:5.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants asked the Buddha,
translation_text.sn6.10:5.2: “Sir, how long is the lifespan in the Pink Lotus hell?”
translation_text.sn6.10:5.5: how many years, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of years it lasts.”
translation_text.sn6.10:5.6: “But sir, is it possible to give a simile?”
translation_text.sn6.10:6.1: “Suppose there was a Kosalan load of twenty packs of sesame seed. And at the end of every hundred years someone would remove a single seed from it.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.2: By this means the Kosalan load of twenty packs of sesame seed would run out faster than a single lifetime in the Abbuda hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.3: Now, twenty lifetimes in the Abbuda hell equal one lifetime in the Nirabbuda hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.4: Twenty lifetimes in the Nirabbuda hell equal one lifetime in the Ababa hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.5: Twenty lifetimes in the Ababa hell equal one lifetime in the Aṭaṭa hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.6: Twenty lifetimes in the Aṭaṭa hell equal one lifetime in the Ahaha hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.7: Twenty lifetimes in the Ahaha hell equal one lifetime in the Yellow Lotus hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.8: Twenty lifetimes in the Yellow Lotus hell equal one lifetime in the Fragrant Water Lily hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.9: Twenty lifetimes in the Fragrant Water Lily hell equal one lifetime in the Blue Water Lily hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.10: Twenty lifetimes in the Blue Water Lily hell equal one lifetime in the White Lotus hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.11: Twenty lifetimes in the White Lotus hell equal one lifetime in the Pink Lotus hell.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.12: The mendicant Kokālika has been reborn in the Pink Lotus hell because of his resentment for Sāriputta and Moggallāna.”
translation_text.sn6.10:6.13: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn6.10:6.14: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn6.10:7.2: with an axe in his mouth.
translation_text.sn6.10:7.3: A fool cuts themselves with it
translation_text.sn6.10:8.1: When you praise someone worthy of criticism,
translation_text.sn6.10:8.2: or criticize someone worthy of praise,
translation_text.sn6.10:8.3: you choose a losing hand with your own mouth:
translation_text.sn6.10:8.4: you’ll never find happiness that way.
translation_text.sn6.10:9.1: A losing hand at dice is a trivial thing,
translation_text.sn6.10:9.2: if all you lose is your money
translation_text.sn6.10:9.3: and all you own, even yourself.
translation_text.sn6.10:9.5: is to hate the holy ones.
translation_text.sn6.10:10.1: For a hundred thousand times a hundred million,
translation_text.sn6.10:10.2: times five hundred and thirty-six times <j>a thousand times ten million years
translation_text.sn6.10:10.3: a slanderer of noble ones goes to hell,
translation_text.sn6.10:10.4: having aimed bad words and thoughts at them.”

variant_text.sn6.10:0.3: Kokālikasutta → dutiyakokālikasuttaṁ (bj, sya1ed, sya2ed); kokālikasuttaṁ (2) (cck, pts2ed); kokāliko (2) (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn6.10:2.2: pīḷakāhi → piḷakāhi (bj, sya1ed, sya2ed, pts1ed, pts2ed)
